name: Gochujang Garlic Eggs
description: Gochujang garlic eggs are considered the arch rival of soy marinated eggs and are an addictive Korean side dish.

steps: Take three green onions and thinly slice them. Separate the white parts and green parts.
Smash three cloves of garlic and finely chop them. Thinly slice some green and red chilies.

For the seasoning paste, in a small container, add 2 tbspoons of gochujang, 2 tbsps of Korean chili pepper flakes, 1 tbsp of soy sauce, 1 tbsp of oyster sauce, half a tablespoon of chicken bouillon powder, half a tablespoon of sugar, 1 tbsp of honey, some black pepper, and give it a good mix.
Fill your pot with eight cups of water and bring it to a boil. Carefully add 12 large eggs and let them cook on a gentle heat for about 7 minutes.
Once time's up, take them out and shock them in some ice water, then peel.
Add 1 tbsp of oil to a large pan and heat over medium-high heat. Add a half pound of ground beef, sear for 1 minute, flip, and cook for 1 to 2 minutes until no longer pink.
Add the white parts of the green onions and garlic, and stir fry for 1 minute.
Reduce heat to low, add the seasoning paste, and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add two cups of water, stir, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-high, add the eggs, and cook down for 7 minutes.
Turn heat off, add green onions, chilies, half a tablespoon of toasted sesame oil, and a generous amount of toasted sesame seeds. Give it a final mix.
